children.heading: From Committee on Finance
children.label: 1 From Committee on Finance
children.text: Five members who are members of the Committee on Finance of the Senate, three from the majority and two from the minority party, to be chosen by such Committee; and

children.heading: From Committee on Ways and Means
children.label: 2 From Committee on Ways and Means
children.text: Five members who are members of the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, three from the majority and two from the minority party, to be chosen by such Committee.

children.heading: General limitation
children.label: 1 General limitation
children.text: No person shall continue to serve as a member of the Joint Committee after he has ceased to be a member of the Committee by which he was chosen, except that—

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: The members chosen by the Committee on Ways and Means who have been reelected to the House of Representatives may continue to serve as members of the Joint Committee notwithstanding the expiration of the Congress.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 1 Effect
children.text: Shall not affect the power of the remaining members to execute the functions of the Joint Committee; and

children.heading: Adjournment or recess of Congress
children.label: A Adjournment or recess of Congress
children.text: In case of a vacancy during an adjournment or recess of Congress for a period of more than 2 weeks, the members of the Joint Committee who are members of the Committee entitled to fill such vacancy may designate a member of such Committee to serve until his successor is chosen by such Committee; and

children.heading: Expiration of Congress
children.label: B Expiration of Congress
children.text: In the case of a vacancy after the expiration of a Congress which would be filled by the Committee on Ways and Means, the members of such Committee who are continuing to serve as members of the Joint Committee may designate a person who, immediately prior to such expiration, was a member of such Committee and who is re-elected to the House of Representatives, to serve until his successor is chosen by such Committee.

children.heading: Allowances
children.label: d Allowances
children.text: The members shall serve without compensation in addition to that received for their services as members of Congress; but they shall be reimbursed for travel, subsistence, and other necessary expenses incurred by them in the performance of the duties vested in the Joint Committee, other than expenses in connection with meetings of the Joint Committee held in the District of Columbia during such times as the Congress is in session.
